steps.text: A confidence interval built from a sample mean and margin of error defines the range of plausible values for the true population mean. The interval spans from (mean − margin of error) to (mean + margin of error).
Given: sample mean = $28.88$ ounces, margin of error = $0.13$ ounce. Lower bound: $28.88 - 0.13 = 28.75$ ounces. Upper bound: $28.88 + 0.13 = 29.01$ ounces.

The interpretation is that plausible values for the true average weight of all bags lie between $28.75$ ounces and $29.01$ ounces. This does not say the true mean IS in the interval with certainty, only that these are plausible values consistent with the sample.
Choice A states plausible values are between $28.75$ and $29.01$ ounces, which exactly matches our calculation. That matches choice A — A is correct.

choices.text: • Plausible values between $28.75$ and $29.01$ ounces matches mean ± margin of error: $28.88 \pm 0.13$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
• This says plausible values are OUTSIDE the interval — the opposite of what a margin of error means. Eliminated.
• This says the mean is less than $28.99$ ounces. But $28.99$ has no special significance here, and margin of error defines an interval around the estimate, not a one-sided bound. Eliminated.
• This says the mean is greater than $28.99$ ounces. The upper bound is $29.01$, not $28.99$, and again the margin of error defines a two-sided interval. Eliminated.
